title: I Don'T Like It
lyrics:
[verse 1]
My feet are cold on the floor!
That fork looks mean, not nice.
The green tree thing is too weird.
I don’t wanna open wide!

[chorus]
I don’t like it!
No, no, no!
I don’t like it!
Go away, broccoli!

[verse 2]
It smells like grass and old socks.
It’s bumpy like a toad.
If I eat it, I’ll turn green!
I’ll hide it in my coat!

[chorus]
I don’t like it!
No, no, no!
I don’t like it!
Go away, broccoli!

[ending]
I’m too tired to fight now…
Maybe just one little bite…
*yawn* Okay… fine… night-night.
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, full musical production, xylophone, bass, and tambourine, 114 BPM, steady reassuring groove, predictable strong beat, clear warm vocal, strong singalong chorus, every word easy to understand
scene: The kitchen tiles are cold under your bare feet at 5:37 PM, the orange plastic fork hovering mid-air as Mom says, "Just one bite of the broccoli, please." You turn your head sharply, lips pressed tight, and the words explode: "I don’t like it!
cover_description: A tiny kid in pajamas stands on a kitchen chair, arms crossed, scowling at a giant, wobbly broccoli floret that’s bigger than their head, while a fork with googly eyes cowers in the background.
